# LITMUS Discovery Study — Reducer Report

*Generated by the discovery-study reducer (DESIGN §17 steps 3–4, §19). Synthesized from all 30 `study/discovery/catalog-*.json` joined to `study/corpus/manifest.json`. Cataloguers were blind to `control_type`; the reducer was not. Every label below is a **reasoner candidate judgment**, not gold — see the caveat at the end.*

---

## 1. Corpus summary (by field × control)

30 papers, 30 catalogs read in full.

| field | n | positive (retracted) | negative (registered report) | none |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| chemistry | 16 | — | — | 16 |
| psychology | 4 | — | 2 (`anon2024-rr`, `druckman2021`) | 2 |
| biology | 3 | 2 (`anon-retracted-oblongifolin`, `anon-retracted-stk39`) | — | 1 |
| medicine | 3 | — | — | 3 |
| economics | 2 | — | — | 2 |
| ml | 1 | — | — | 1 |
| physics | 1 | — | — | 1 |
| **total** | **30** | **2** | **2** | **26** |

**Critical corpus-hygiene finding.** The two **positive controls** are not the flawed research articles — they are their **one-page retraction notices** (`biology-anon-retracted-oblongifolin-gem-resistance`, `biology-anon-retracted-stk39-osteosarcoma`). They contain no figures, tables, statistics, or SI from the retracted studies, only the editor's notice. Their only auditable content is **integrity signals** (declared image/data duplication, route-to-human) plus trivial within-notice metadata consistency (e.g. a cited figure page falling inside the article's page span). The retracted *data* is absent from the corpus. If the intent of the positive controls was to test whether the auditor catches fabricated/manipulated *results*, that signal is not recoverable from these files — the original article PDFs (DOIs `10.3892/etm.2020.8856`, `10.3892/ol.2017.6728`) would have to be supplied. A cheap **retraction-notice / document-type detector** should pre-filter these so the pipeline never scores a notice as a primary paper.

Other corpus-metadata oddities (labeling notes, not result problems): the slug `druckman2021` is actually a **political-science** paper authored solely by Moniz (Druckman appears only in its citations); `chemistry-roy2025` is authored by Subramaniyan/Hering-Junghans/Barath (2026); the `druckman2021` paper is filed under field=psychology though it is experimental political science.

---

## 2. Issue archetypes (frequency-ranked)

Full structured list in `archetypes.json` (16 archetypes). Frequencies are reducer tallies of claims whose checkable **form** matches the archetype (one claim may match several), not adjudicated counts.

| # | archetype | tier | freq (claims) | fields | verifier | prebuilt match |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | prose-vs-table value disagreement | T2 | ~95 | all 7 | prebuild | `prose_vs_table` |
| 2 | internal arithmetic recompute (sums/%/ratio/stoich) | T0 | ~88 | all 7 | prebuild | `sum_check` |
| 3 | prose-vs-figure value extraction (read plot, then check) | T2 | ~78 | all 7 | prebuild* | `figure_vs_table` |
| 4 | method-appropriateness judgment | T3 | ~52 | chem/med/psych/econ | synthesize | — |
| 5 | claim-vs-evidence support / over-reach | T4 | ~44 | all | synthesize | — |
| 6 | statcheck (p vs F/t/χ²) | T0 | ~40 | psych/med/econ | prebuild | `statcheck` |
| 7 | external-literature / citation fidelity | T5 | ~40 | chem/med/psych | synthesize | — |
| 8 | fixed-knowledge constant / nomenclature / standard | T1 | ~38 | chem/phys/econ/med | synthesize | `unit_consistency` (partial) |
| 9 | unit / dimensional consistency | T0 | ~30 | chem/phys/econ | prebuild | `unit_consistency` |
| 10 | reproducibility recompute from SI/data | T6 | ~30 | chem/econ/psych | synthesize (deferred) | — |
| 11 | cross-table / cross-section value conflict | T2 | ~24 | ml/psych/med/chem | prebuild | `prose_vs_table` |
| 12 | equation / mechanism / cycle recompute | T0 | ~22 | chem/phys/econ-theory | synthesize | — |
| 13 | value-bounds plausibility (GRIM/impossible) | T2 | ~20 | psych/med/econ | prebuild | `grim` |
| 14 | CI-symmetry & effect-size coherence | T0 | ~18 | psych/med | synthesize | `statcheck` (partial) |
| 15 | sample-flow / df-budget / CONSORT reconciliation | T0 | ~16 | med/psych | synthesize | — |
| 16 | integrity / provenance signal (route-to-human) | T7 | ~28 | bio/chem/psych | synthesize | — |

\* figure-reading exists as `figure_vs_table` but its **reliability is the binding constraint** (see §4).

**Field texture.** The corpus splits into two checkable cultures:
- **Chemistry / materials / physics / comp-chem / ml** → arithmetic, stoichiometry/mass-balance, unit conversions, equation/cycle recomputes, and **figure value-extraction**. No `statcheck` claims (no p/F/t triples). The richest deterministic frontier is equation/cycle recompute from SI total-energy tables (`qmc-orr`, `pem-fuelcell`) and figure-slope/peak reads (`physics-singh`).
- **Psychology / medicine / economics** → `statcheck`, CI-symmetry, effect-size coherence, GRIM bounds, and sample-flow/df budgets. The dominant defect class here is **statistical transcription error** (p written into the χ² slot; impossible F with a significant p; asymmetric CIs; df off-by-one).

---

## 3. Verifier coverage map (what to prebuild next)

Full map in `coverage-map.json`. Against the current set `{sum_check, percent_change, yield_check, mass_balance, unit_consistency, statcheck, grim, prose_vs_table, figure_vs_table}`:

**EXISTS (current verifiers cover the largest real-flag volume).** `prose_vs_table` (most frequent archetype), `statcheck` (highest-yield social/medical), `percent_change`, `sum_check`, `mass_balance`, `yield_check`, `grim`, `unit_consistency`. These nine collectively catch the bulk of the confirmable suspect flags.

**Highest-value to harden next — `figure_vs_table` (figure value-extraction).** It gates the single largest block of T2 claims and the **load-bearing** physics figure-vs-prose contradictions (`singh2026` Fig 3a slope `m=2000` vs prose "approximately unity"; Ashby chart using an unmeasured Seebeck and a κ inconsistent with the headline 0.088). Its reliability bounds corpus-wide recall.

**Top GAPs to synthesize, by payoff:**
1. **sample-flow / df-budget / CONSORT reconciliation** — cheap, deterministic; catches `anchuri2026` impossible wallet-day panel (35.3M obs > 16.2M ceiling), `anon2024-rr` df-budget 507>506 and within-model df 133-vs-381, `lin2025` CONSORT split 7/4/4 vs text 6/4/3/2.
2. **CI-symmetry & effect-size coherence** — cheap, deterministic; catches what `statcheck` misses: asymmetric CIs (`anon2024-rr#c4`), η²ₚ=0.28 vs F=66.96 (`wheeler2025#c9`), power 77% inconsistent with Cohen's h=0.95 (`lin2025#c5`), a Clopper-Pearson CI matching no standard method (`lin2025#c25`).
3. **equation / symbolic / thermodynamic-cycle recompute** — high value in comp-chem/physics/econ-theory; needs a CAS (sympy) backend + a "cycle recompute from SI total-energy tables" template (`qmc-orr`, `pem-fuelcell`).
4. **fixed-knowledge constant / nomenclature DB** (CODATA/NIST/CTCAE/JEL) — `unit_consistency` does the conversion but not the lookup (Au work-function mislabeled "electron affinity"; `[NTf2]` spelled as triflate; an EDS "Pd line" at 3.382 keV that does not exist; CTCAE alopecia capped at Grade 2).

**Deferred until artifact access (T5/T6):** reproducibility recompute from deposited data/SI; external-literature/citation fidelity; forensic image-duplication (needs the original figures).

**Advisory, route-to-human (never an A/B verdict):** method-appropriateness; claim-vs-evidence over-reach; integrity/provenance signals.

---

## 4. Candidate benchmark + caveat

`candidate-benchmark.json` lists **91 items** across **24 of 30 papers** (papers without a concrete deterministic suspect/anchor — the two clean mehta owner-papers, water-nanodroplet, pzt-pfn, fecconib, chiral-2d — are not represented). Every deterministic **suspect** flag is included, plus **supported anchors** to calibrate. Each item carries `paper_id`, `claim_id`, `candidate_label` (supported|suspect), `tier`, `control_type`, `needs_confirmation:true`.

Most decisive candidate-suspect flags (adjudicate first):

- **`wheeler2025` c1/c2/c3** — all three baseline χ² tests print the *p-value in the χ² slot* (χ²=0.72 recomputes to 5.36, etc.); systematic; a contingency/statcheck recompute settles it.
- **`anon2024-rr` c1** — F(1,188)=1.58 with p<0.001 is impossible (η²ₚ=0.17 implies F≈38.5); a cluster of 6–7 transcription errors in this **negative-control** registered report.
- **`lin2025` c16/c17** — alopecia listed as a Grade-3 event (abstract) and drawn at 100% red/Grade-3 (Fig 3) while Table 3 + text say Grade 2 / Grade 3 = 0; NCI-CTCAE caps alopecia at Grade 2, so the Grade-3 depiction is *externally impossible*.
- **`anchuri2026` c27** — L1 wallet-day observations (35,276,914) exceed the hard panel ceiling wallets×periods (16,223,885); a pure bounds check.
- **`physics-singh` c4/c3/c8** — Fig 3a slope annotated `m=2000` vs prose "approximately unity"; Ashby-chart placement inconsistent with the measured κ and using an unmeasured Seebeck; prose XRD peaks not matching the labeled strongest peaks. Plus a **blank DFT methodology section** (integrity/completeness signal).
- **`mace-finetuning` c1** — Table 2 "All Reactions" total 1569 vs row-sum 1739 (column-total check).
- **`ml-xu` c5** — Muris-Liver NMI is 88.38 in Table 2 but 83.88 in Table 3 for the *same* model+dataset (identical SD) — cross-table identity.
- **`oztoksoy` c2** — crystallinity Xc,PP for rPP7 reproduces *only* by (incorrectly) using rPP5's weight fraction — a clean templated-arithmetic catch.

### Positive/negative-control sanity check (DESIGN §17)

**Do candidate-suspect claims concentrate in the positive (retracted) controls vs the negative (registered-report) controls? No — and the reason is structural, so this is a signal about the corpus/control *design*, not a verdict on any paper.**

1. The two **positive controls** are one-page **retraction notices** with no auditable results, so their "flaws" are integrity signals (declared image duplication, route-to-human), not recomputable suspects. The retracted data is not in the corpus at all.
2. The two **negative controls** behave *oppositely* to the naive expectation at the deterministic frontier: `druckman2021` (registered report) is **clean** — no fabricated errors, all anchors supported, Bayes factors used appropriately. But `anon2024-rr` (also a registered report) carries a **cluster** of statcheck/CI/df transcription errors (c1–c7), even though its central preregistered *null* conclusion is internally sound. So "high-rigor / registered report" did not imply "error-free at the reporting level."
3. The recomputable suspect flags therefore concentrate in the **`none`-control** papers — `wheeler2025`, `lin2025`, `gkekas2025`, `anchuri2026`, `physics-singh`, and the chemistry set — not in the controls.

**Implication for the study design:** to validate the reward function's discrimination, the corpus needs (a) the *original* retracted article PDFs (not just notices) for the positive controls, and (b) awareness that a registered-report negative control can still be riddled with transcription errors the deterministic frontier will (correctly) flag even when the science is sound. The errors found are predominantly **transcription/typographical**, not fabrication — none individually overturns the headline of the paper that carries it.

### Caveat

**Candidate labels are reasoner judgments, not gold.** A claim becomes gold only once a deterministic verifier recomputes it or a human confirms it (DESIGN §17). The `suspect`/`supported` tags here are first-pass triage to seed the benchmark and prioritize the verifier queue — not verdicts on the papers.

---

- `study/discovery/archetypes.json` — 16 issue archetypes, frequency-ranked, with verifier recommendations.
- `study/discovery/coverage-map.json` — 18 capabilities mapped to exists/gap + queue-priority summary.
- `study/discovery/candidate-benchmark.json` — 91 candidate benchmark items across 24 papers.
- `study/discovery/REPORT.md` — this report.
